title: Liquid Glass OS-screenshot capture blocker — definitive root cause
summary: The 25 visualTierDebt surfaces cannot receive osScreenshotProof because the macOS login session is LOCKED (CGSSessionScreenIsLocked=1), not because of any window-id capture API limitation. While locked, the user session's windows (including the non-activating Script Kit NSPanel) are not composited to the physical display, so screencapture only sees the lock screen. This is identical for ScreenCaptureKit — locked-session content is uncapturable. No code change can resolve it; the display must be UNLOCKED and awake.

mechanismValidated.note: The capture MECHANISM is solved and proven. The dead path (window-id CGWindowListCreateImage -> 'could not create image from window') and the obsoleted CGDisplayCreateImage are both bypassed by: caffeinate -u (wake) -> screencapture -D<displayIndex> (full display, real compositor output) -> sips crop to the window rect via tahoe_window_geometry.

validatedCapture.caveat: 0.9939 non-black content was the LOCK SCREEN behind the panel region, proving the capture+crop pipeline works end-to-end; real panel content requires an unlocked session.

tooling.lockAware: tahoe-oscapture.sh refuses to fabricate proof: it writes a `blocked` receipt (countsAsOsScreenshotEvidence=false) and exits 2 when CGSSessionScreenIsLocked=1, the window is absent, or nonBlackRatio < 0.01.

preconditions: Unlock the Mac (Touch ID / password) so CGSSessionScreenIsLocked=0
Keep the display awake (the wrapper holds caffeinate -u during capture)
howToFinish.perSurface: scripts/devtools/tahoe-oscapture.sh <term> <SurfaceKind>  (drive each surface visible first via session.sh + triggerBuiltin/launcher path)
howToFinish.expectedFlip: Each captured surface: osScreenshotProof blocked->pass; when imageDiffProof also passes -> strong-proof; visualTierDebtSurfaceCount -> 0; classification incomplete -> ok.
